annotations.label: Weierstrass approximation theorem
annotations.note: States that the polynomial functions on `[a,b]` are dense in `C(Icc a b, ℝ)`, exactly the Weierstrass approximation theorem.

annotations.label: Jackson's inequality

anchor.snippet: Jackson's inequality bounds the error of approximations by polynomials of a given degree

annotations.note: No declaration mentioning Jackson's inequality was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Arbitrarily slow convergence for continuous functions

anchor.snippet: the convergence of the approximations can be arbitrarily slow in the following sense

annotations.note: No matching arbitrarily-slow-convergence result located in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Separability of C[a,b]

anchor.snippet: one can show that the space C[ a , b ] is separable

annotations.note: The `SeparableSpace C(X, Y)` instance for second-countable locally compact X and second-countable Y specializes to `C([a,b], ℝ)`.

annotations.label: Banach algebra C[a,b] with supremum norm

anchor.snippet: The set C[ a , b ] of continuous real-valued functions on [ a , b ] , together with the supremum norm

annotations.note: The supremum-norm `NormedRing`/`NormedAlgebra` structure on `C(α, γ)` for compact α is provided via instances in `Compact.lean`.

annotations.label: Separates points

anchor.snippet: a set A of functions defined on X is said to separate points if

annotations.note: `Set.SeparatesPoints` (Logic.Function.Basic) and `Subalgebra.SeparatesPoints` directly capture this definition.

annotations.label: Stone–Weierstrass theorem (real numbers)
annotations.note: States that a point-separating subalgebra of `C(X, ℝ)` for compact X is dense, the real Stone-Weierstrass theorem.

annotations.label: Functions vanishing at infinity

anchor.snippet: be the space of real-valued continuous functions on X that vanish at infinity

annotations.note: The `C₀(α, β)` type bundles continuous maps that tend to 0 along the cocompact filter.

annotations.label: Subalgebra vanishes nowhere

anchor.snippet: is said to vanish nowhere if not all of the elements

annotations.note: No `VanishesNowhere`/`vanishesNowhere` predicate was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stone–Weierstrass theorem (locally compact spaces)
annotations.note: Mathlib has an `ε`-approximation variant for non-compact X on a compact subset, but the C₀ formulation for locally compact spaces is explicitly listed as future work in the file.

annotations.label: Polynomial approximation in two variables

anchor.snippet: If f is a continuous real-valued function defined on the set

annotations.note: No dedicated bivariate polynomial-approximation lemma, but the result follows from the general real Stone-Weierstrass theorem in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Approximation by sums of products on X×Y

anchor.snippet: If X and Y are two compact Hausdorff spaces and f

annotations.note: Mathlib has no explicit statement about density of sums of products `g(x)h(y)` in `C(X×Y, ℝ)`, but it is an immediate corollary of the general real Stone-Weierstrass theorem.

annotations.label: Complex unital *-algebra generated

anchor.snippet: consists of all those functions that can be obtained from the elements of

annotations.note: `StarSubalgebra.adjoin` (and `StarAlgebra.adjoin`) defines the unital *-algebra generated by a set.

annotations.label: Fourier series basis on the circle

anchor.snippet: Fourier series : The set of linear combinations of functions

annotations.note: Proves the star subalgebra of `C(AddCircle T, ℂ)` generated by `fourier n` is dense, which is the Stone-Weierstrass corollary for the circle.

annotations.label: Stone–Weierstrass theorem (complex numbers)
annotations.note: Stated for any `RCLike 𝕜` (including ℂ): a point-separating star subalgebra of `C(X, 𝕜)` for compact X is dense.

annotations.label: Stone–Weierstrass theorem (quaternion numbers)
annotations.note: Mathlib's Stone-Weierstrass is stated only for `RCLike` (ℝ/ℂ), not for quaternion-valued continuous functions.

annotations.label: Non-commutative Stone–Weierstrass conjecture
annotations.note: An open conjecture for non-commutative C*-algebras; no formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stone–Weierstrass theorem (C*-algebras)
annotations.note: The commutative C*-algebra Stone-Weierstrass is exactly the star-subalgebra version for `C(X, 𝕜)` with `RCLike 𝕜`, used e.g. in `GelfandDuality`.

annotations.label: Lattice in C(X,R)

anchor.snippet: A subset L of C( X , R ) is called a lattice if for any two elements

annotations.note: The lattice structure on `C(α, β)` (with sup/inf inherited pointwise) is provided; sub-lattice membership is then standard `Set` notion.

annotations.label: Characterization of closure in a lattice

anchor.snippet: The function φ ∈ C( X , R ) belongs to the closure of L if and only if

annotations.note: No explicit pointwise-ε characterization of the closure of a sublattice was located in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Stone–Weierstrass theorem (lattices)
annotations.note: Proves a nonempty sublattice of `C(X, ℝ)` which separates points strongly has closure equal to ⊤, the lattice Stone-Weierstrass theorem.

annotations.label: Stone–Weierstrass theorem (max-closed)
annotations.note: No max-closed (only-sup) variant of Stone-Weierstrass was found in Mathlib.
